title: Fixed synthetic AP triage workflow-model comparison

registration.timing: The plan is frozen before generating the derived results files, but after the underlying developer regression fixtures and their deterministic outcomes already existed.
registration.notClaimed: This is not an external, prospective, outcome-blind preregistration.

researchQuestion: On a fixed synthetic AP case set, how do a manual checklist workflow model and an Autopilot-assisted review workflow model compare on modeled active-review time, modeled human touches, and policy-label mismatches?

caseSet.selectionRule: A fixed, developer-selected stratified subset spanning clean new-vendor, clean recurring-vendor, missing-field, unreconciled, duplicate, anomaly, precedence, currency-change, and no-total paths.
caseSet.labelAuthority: Developer-authored AP policy labels from the tuned regression set; not independent expert ground truth.
caseSet.exclusionsAfterFreeze: None. Missing, malformed, or duplicate observations fail analysis; no case is dropped or imputed.

manual.name: Manual checklist workflow model
manual.definition: A synthetic first-pass AP clerk checklist: read, normalize, validate required fields, attempt reconciliation, retrieve history, apply history checks when history exists, choose the policy route, and record a rationale.
manual.decisionSource: Frozen developer application of that checklist to each authored case. No person was timed or observed.
manual.timeMeasure: Modeled active-review seconds from the task weights below; not elapsed time.
manual.touchMeasure: Count of preregistered human checkpoints in the listed task sequence.

assisted.name: Autopilot-assisted review workflow model
assisted.definition: The agent performs normalization/evidence gathering and proposes a route; the synthetic human workflow reviews normalized fields, inspects the trace, inspects exception detail when present, confirms the policy route, and approves or defers.
assisted.decisionSource: Frozen deterministic offline replay through eval/lib.ts runScenario with FakeQwenChatClient. The replay shares the production tool-call parsing seam but is not a live-Qwen or production observation.
assisted.timeMeasure: Modeled human active-review seconds only. Model latency, queue time, and downstream execution time are excluded.
assisted.touchMeasure: Count of preregistered human review checkpoints in the listed task sequence.

primary.derivation: For each arm and case, sum the base seconds of every listed human task; paired delta is manual minus assisted.
For each arm and case, sum humanTouches for every listed task; paired delta is manual minus assisted.

safety.derivation: Action differs from the developer-authored expectedAction for that case.

analysisPlan.missingData: fail closed; no exclusions or imputation
analysisPlan.rounding: Counts and totals remain integers; means, medians, rates, and percentages round to one decimal place.
analysisPlan.baseComparison: Sum and compare base task weights within each paired case, then aggregate all 12 fixed cases.
analysisPlan.sensitivity: Report a deliberately adverse bound (manual low minus assisted high) and favorable bound (manual high minus assisted low). These bounds explore only the declared task-weight assumptions; they are not confidence intervals.
analysisPlan.subgroups: Report descriptive category aggregates only; no subgroup significance tests.
analysisPlan.replayValidation: Every check and write reruns all 12 selected eval/dataset.ts scenarios through eval/lib.ts runScenario in offline mode and requires final action, reported model id, raw model action, stop reason, read/analyze steps, trace tools, and policyOverride to match the frozen raw observation. Local latency is excluded.
analysisPlan.runtimeAndSourceIdentity: Every check and write requires process.version v24.18.0, a committed raw evidence input, and the complete local replay closure: src; all transitively loaded eval modules; the analyzer/provenance gate; protocol/case inputs; and package, lock, and TypeScript manifests. Eval replay modules are dynamically imported only after those gates. Source refresh requires a completely clean committed HEAD (commit A), records that exact commit/tree, reruns all 12 cases, and produces only raw/results evidence for descendant commit B. Later evidence-only commits are allowed and are not serialized into generated output.
analysisPlan.inference: No p-values, confidence intervals, causal claims, population estimates, or production generalization.

read_invoice.description: Open and read the incoming invoice.

normalize_fields.description: Normalize aliases, types, dates, currency, and amounts.

reconcile_amounts.description: Attempt subtotal, tax, total, and line reconciliation.

retrieve_vendor_history.description: Search prior completed invoices and corrections.

check_duplicate.description: Compare reference, vendor, date, and amount against history.

compare_amount_and_currency.description: Compare amount range and currency against vendor history.

choose_policy_route.description: Select accrue, pay, query, or specialist review.

review_normalized_invoice.description: Review the normalized invoice fields shown by Autopilot.

inspect_agent_evidence.description: Inspect the auditable tool trace and validation findings.

inspect_exception_detail.description: Inspect the specific exception, collision, or ambiguous evidence.

confirm_policy_route.description: Confirm or correct the proposed AP route.

approve_or_defer.description: Approve the exact proposal or defer it for follow-up.

assumptions: The task list is a workflow model, not a time-and-motion observation.
Task weights are authored assumptions chosen before derived-result generation and are visible for replacement by future measured values.
Every task represents active human review; system latency and downstream execution are excluded.
The manual arm is a reasonable standardized checklist and is not intentionally deprived of history, duplicate, or variance checks.
Both actions and labels are developer-authored, so mismatch counts are a policy-regression signal rather than an estimate of human or production error.
The 12 cases are synthetic and developer-selected, not random, representative, independent, or production sampled.
